Ohigginsia discolor is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ohigginsia discolor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is often used to make various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are traditionally consumed to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
